seoTitle: How to Add Swimlanes in Jira Boards
metaDescription: Learn how to add and configure swimlanes in Jira to group issues by epics, assignees, or JQL for a more organized board.
intro: A crowded board without any structure makes it hard to see who's working on what. This guide covers adding swimlanes to a Jira board, choosing a grouping strategy, and configuring the settings so your Scrum or Kanban board stays organized.

steps.heading: Open board settings
steps.body: Navigate to your board's settings to access the swimlane configuration options.

steps.heading: Configure swimlanes
steps.body: Choose the Swimlanes tab within board settings to start setting up how you want to group your issues.

steps.heading: Group issues by epics or JQL
steps.body: Select a grouping strategy — such as by epic, assignee, or a custom JQL query — depending on how you want the board organized.

steps.heading: Test and save changes
steps.body: Apply your changes and check the board to confirm the swimlanes are grouping issues the way you expect.

faq.question: How do I add swimlanes to a Jira board?
faq.answer: Open board settings, go to the Swimlanes tab, and choose a grouping strategy like epics, assignees, or JQL.

faq.question: Can I group swimlanes using a custom JQL query?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira supports custom JQL-based swimlanes for more precise grouping beyond the default options.

faq.question: Does adding swimlanes work on both Scrum and Kanban boards?
faq.answer: Yes, swimlanes can be configured on both Scrum and Kanban boards through the board settings.

faq.question: Why would I use swimlanes instead of just filtering the board?
faq.answer: Swimlanes let you see multiple groupings at once within the same view, while filters only narrow down what's shown.
